15	/// AXI4+ATOP to banked SRAM memory slave. Allows for parallel read and write transactions.
16	/// Has higher throughput than `axi_to_mem`, however needs more hardware.
17	///
18	/// The used address space starts at 0x0 and ends at the capacity of all memory banks combined.
19	/// The higher address bits are ignored for accesses.
20	module axi_to_mem_banked #(
21	  /// AXI4+ATOP ID width
22	  parameter int unsigned                  AxiIdWidth    = 32'd0,
23	  /// AXI4+ATOP address width
24	  parameter int unsigned                  AxiAddrWidth  = 32'd0,
25	  /// AXI4+ATOP data width
26	  parameter int unsigned                  AxiDataWidth  = 32'd0,
27	  /// AXI4+ATOP AW channel struct
28	  parameter type                          axi_aw_chan_t = logic,
29	  /// AXI4+ATOP  W channel struct
30	  parameter type                          axi_w_chan_t  = logic,
31	  /// AXI4+ATOP  B channel struct
32	  parameter type                          axi_b_chan_t  = logic,
33	  /// AXI4+ATOP AR channel struct
34	  parameter type                          axi_ar_chan_t = logic,
35	  /// AXI4+ATOP  R channel struct
36	  parameter type                          axi_r_chan_t  = logic,
37	  /// AXI4+ATOP request struct
38	  parameter type                          axi_req_t     = logic,
39	  /// AXI4+ATOP response struct
40	  parameter type                          axi_resp_t    = logic,
41	  /// Number of memory banks / macros
42	  /// Has to satisfy:
43	  /// - MemNumBanks >= 2 * AxiDataWidth / MemDataWidth
44	  /// - MemNumBanks is a power of 2.
45	  parameter int unsigned                  MemNumBanks   = 32'd4,
46	  /// Address width of an individual memory bank. This is treated as a word address.
47	  parameter int unsigned                  MemAddrWidth  = 32'd11,
48	  /// Data width of the memory macros.
49	  /// Has to satisfy:
50	  /// - AxiDataWidth % MemDataWidth = 0
51	  parameter int unsigned                  MemDataWidth  = 32'd32,
52	  /// Read latency of the connected memory in cycles
53	  parameter int unsigned                  MemLatency    = 32'd1,
54	  /// Hide write requests if the strb == '0
55	  parameter bit                           HideStrb      = 1'b0,
56	  /// Depth of output fifo/fall_through_register. Increase for asymmetric backpressure (contention) on banks.
57	  parameter int unsigned                  OutFifoDepth = 1,
58	  /// DEPENDENT PARAMETER, DO NOT OVERWRITE! Address type of the memory request.
59	  parameter type mem_addr_t = logic [MemAddrWidth-1:0],
60	  /// DEPENDENT PARAMETER, DO NOT OVERWRITE! Atomic operation type for the memory request.
61	  parameter type mem_atop_t = axi_pkg::atop_t,
62	  /// DEPENDENT PARAMETER, DO NOT OVERWRITE! Data type for the memory request.
63	  parameter type mem_data_t = logic [MemDataWidth-1:0],
64	  /// DEPENDENT PARAMETER, DO NOT OVERWRITE! Byte strobe/enable signal for the memory request.
65	  parameter type mem_strb_t = logic [MemDataWidth/8-1:0]
66	) (
67	  /// Clock
68	  input  logic                        clk_i,
69	  /// Asynchronous reset, active low
70	  input  logic                        rst_ni,
71	  /// Testmode enable
72	  input  logic                        test_i,
73	  /// AXI4+ATOP slave port, request struct
74	  input  axi_req_t                    axi_req_i,
75	  /// AXI4+ATOP slave port, response struct
76	  output axi_resp_t                   axi_resp_o,
77	  /// Memory bank request
78	  output logic      [MemNumBanks-1:0] mem_req_o,
79	  /// Memory request grant
80	  input  logic      [MemNumBanks-1:0] mem_gnt_i,
81	  /// Request address
82	  output mem_addr_t [MemNumBanks-1:0] mem_add_o,
83	  /// Write request enable, active high
84	  output logic      [MemNumBanks-1:0] mem_we_o,
85	  /// Write data
86	  output mem_data_t [MemNumBanks-1:0] mem_wdata_o,
87	  /// Write data byte enable, active high
88	  output mem_strb_t [MemNumBanks-1:0] mem_be_o,
89	  /// Atomic operation
90	  output mem_atop_t [MemNumBanks-1:0] mem_atop_o,
91	  /// Read data response
92	  input  mem_data_t [MemNumBanks-1:0] mem_rdata_i,
93	  /// Status output, busy flag of `axi_to_mem`
94	  output logic      [1:0]             axi_to_mem_busy_o
95	);
96	  /// This specifies the number of banks needed to have the full data bandwidth of one
97	  /// AXI data channel.
98	  localparam int unsigned BanksPerAxiChannel = AxiDataWidth / MemDataWidth;
99	  /// Offset of the byte address from AXI to determine, where the selection signal for the
100	  /// memory bank should start.
101	  localparam int unsigned BankSelOffset = $clog2(MemDataWidth / 32'd8);
102	  /// Selection signal width of the xbar. This is the reason for power of two banks, otherwise
103	  /// There are holes in the address mapping.
104	  localparam int unsigned BankSelWidth  = cf_math_pkg::idx_width(MemNumBanks);
105	  typedef logic [BankSelWidth-1:0] xbar_sel_t;
106	
107	  // Typedef for defining the channels
108	  typedef enum logic {
109	    ReadAccess  = 1'b0,
110	    WriteAccess = 1'b1
111	  } access_type_e;
112	  typedef logic [AxiAddrWidth-1:0] axi_addr_t;
113	
114	  /// Payload definition which is sent over the xbar between the macros and the read/write unit.
115	  typedef struct packed {
116	    /// Address for the memory access
117	    mem_addr_t addr;
118	    /// Write enable, active high
119	    logic      we;
120	    /// Write data
121	    mem_data_t wdata;
122	    /// Strobe signal, byte enable
123	    mem_strb_t wstrb;
124	    /// Atomic operation, from AXI
125	    mem_atop_t atop;
126	  } xbar_payload_t;
127	
128	  /// Read data definition for the shift register, which samples the read response data
129	  typedef struct packed {
130	    /// Selection signal for response routing
131	    xbar_sel_t sel;
132	    /// Selection is valid
133	    logic      valid;
134	  } read_sel_t;
135	
136	  axi_req_t  [1:0] mem_axi_reqs;
137	  axi_resp_t [1:0] mem_axi_resps;
138	
139	  // Fixed select `axi_demux` to split reads and writes to the two `axi_to_mem`
140	  axi_demux #(
141	    .AxiIdWidth  ( AxiIdWidth    ),
142	    .AtopSupport ( 1'b1          ),
143	    .aw_chan_t   ( axi_aw_chan_t ),
144	    .w_chan_t    ( axi_w_chan_t  ),
145	    .b_chan_t    ( axi_b_chan_t  ),
146	    .ar_chan_t   ( axi_ar_chan_t ),
147	    .r_chan_t    ( axi_r_chan_t  ),
148	    .axi_req_t   ( axi_req_t     ),
149	    .axi_resp_t  ( axi_resp_t    ),
150	    .NoMstPorts  ( 32'd2         ),
151	    .MaxTrans    ( MemLatency+2  ), // allow multiple Ax vectors to not starve W channel
152	    .AxiLookBits ( 32'd1         ), // select is fixed, do not need it
153	    .UniqueIds   ( 1'b1          ), // Can be set as ports are statically selected -> reduces HW
154	    .SpillAw     ( 1'b1          ),
155	    .SpillW      ( 1'b1          ),
156	    .SpillB      ( 1'b1          ),
157	    .SpillAr     ( 1'b1          ),
158	    .SpillR      ( 1'b1          )
159	  ) i_axi_demux (
160	    .clk_i,
161	    .rst_ni,
162	    .test_i,
163	    .slv_req_i       ( axi_req_i     ),
164	    .slv_aw_select_i ( WriteAccess   ),
165	    .slv_ar_select_i ( ReadAccess    ),
166	    .slv_resp_o      ( axi_resp_o    ),
167	    .mst_reqs_o      ( mem_axi_reqs  ),
168	    .mst_resps_i     ( mem_axi_resps )
169	  );
170	
171	  xbar_payload_t [1:0][BanksPerAxiChannel-1:0] inter_payload;
172	  xbar_sel_t     [1:0][BanksPerAxiChannel-1:0] inter_sel;
173	  logic          [1:0][BanksPerAxiChannel-1:0] inter_valid,   inter_ready;
174	
175	  // axi_to_mem protocol converter
176	  for (genvar i = 0; i < 2; i++) begin : gen_axi_to_mem
177	    axi_addr_t [BanksPerAxiChannel-1:0] req_addr;  // This is a byte address
178	    mem_data_t [BanksPerAxiChannel-1:0] req_wdata, res_rdata;
179	    mem_strb_t [BanksPerAxiChannel-1:0] req_wstrb;
180	    mem_atop_t [BanksPerAxiChannel-1:0] req_atop;
181	
182	    logic      [BanksPerAxiChannel-1:0] req_we,    res_valid;
183	
184	    // Careful, request / grant
185	    // Only assert grant, if there is a ready
186	    axi_to_mem #(
187	      .axi_req_t    ( axi_req_t          ),
188	      .axi_resp_t   ( axi_resp_t         ),
189	      .AddrWidth    ( AxiAddrWidth       ),
190	      .DataWidth    ( AxiDataWidth       ),
191	      .IdWidth      ( AxiIdWidth         ),
192	      .NumBanks     ( BanksPerAxiChannel ),
193	      .BufDepth     ( MemLatency         ),
194	      .HideStrb     ( HideStrb           ),
195	      .OutFifoDepth ( OutFifoDepth       )
196	    ) i_axi_to_mem (
197	      .clk_i,
198	      .rst_ni,
199	      .busy_o       ( axi_to_mem_busy_o[i]            ),
200	      .axi_req_i    ( mem_axi_reqs[i]                 ),
201	      .axi_resp_o   ( mem_axi_resps[i]                ),
202	      .mem_req_o    ( inter_valid[i]                  ),
203	      .mem_gnt_i    ( inter_ready[i] & inter_valid[i] ), // convert valid/ready to req/gnt
204	      .mem_addr_o   ( req_addr                        ),
205	      .mem_wdata_o  ( req_wdata                       ),
206	      .mem_strb_o   ( req_wstrb                       ),
207	      .mem_atop_o   ( req_atop                        ),
208	      .mem_we_o     ( req_we                          ),
209	      .mem_rvalid_i ( res_valid                       ),
210	      .mem_rdata_i  ( res_rdata                       )
211	    );
212	    // Pack the payload data together
213	    for (genvar j = 0; unsigned'(j) < BanksPerAxiChannel; j++) begin : gen_response_mux
214	      // Cut out the bank selection signal.
215	      assign inter_sel[i][j] = req_addr[j][BankSelOffset+:BankSelWidth];
216	
217	      // Assign the xbar payload.
218	      assign inter_payload[i][j] = xbar_payload_t'{
219	        // Cut out the word address for the banks.
220	        addr:    req_addr[j][(BankSelOffset+BankSelWidth)+:MemAddrWidth],
221	        we:      req_we[j],
222	        wdata:   req_wdata[j],
223	        wstrb:   req_wstrb[j],
224	        atop:    req_atop[j],
225	        default: '0
226	      };
227	
228	      // Cut out the portion of the address for the bank selection, each bank is word addressed!
229	      read_sel_t r_shift_inp, r_shift_oup;
230	      // Pack the selection into the shift register
231	      assign r_shift_inp = read_sel_t'{
232	        sel:     inter_sel[i][j],                       // Selection for response multiplexer
233	        valid:   inter_valid[i][j] & inter_ready[i][j], // Valid when req to SRAM
234	        default: '0
235	      };
236	
237	      // Select the right read response data.
238	      // Writes should also generate a `response`.
239	      assign res_valid[j] = r_shift_oup.valid;
240	      assign res_rdata[j] = mem_rdata_i[r_shift_oup.sel];
241	
242	      // Connect for the response data `MemLatency` cycles after a request was made to the xbar.
243	      shift_reg #(
244	        .dtype ( read_sel_t ),
245	        .Depth ( MemLatency )
246	      ) i_shift_reg_rdata_mux (
247	        .clk_i,
248	        .rst_ni,
249	        .d_i    ( r_shift_inp ),
250	        .d_o    ( r_shift_oup )
251	      );
252	    end
253	  end
254	
255	  // Xbar to arbitrate data over the different memory banks
256	  xbar_payload_t [MemNumBanks-1:0] mem_payload;
257	
258	  stream_xbar #(
259	    .NumInp      ( 32'd2 * BanksPerAxiChannel ),
260	    .NumOut      ( MemNumBanks                ),
261	    .payload_t   ( xbar_payload_t             ),
262	    .OutSpillReg ( 1'b0                       ),
263	    .ExtPrio     ( 1'b0                       ),
264	    .AxiVldRdy   ( 1'b1                       ),
265	    .LockIn      ( 1'b1                       )
266	  ) i_stream_xbar (
267	    .clk_i,
268	    .rst_ni,
269	    .flush_i ( 1'b0          ),
270	    .rr_i    ( '0            ),
271	    .data_i  ( inter_payload ),
272	    .sel_i   ( inter_sel     ),
273	    .valid_i ( inter_valid   ),
274	    .ready_o ( inter_ready   ),
275	    .data_o  ( mem_payload   ),
276	    .idx_o   ( /*not used*/  ),
277	    .valid_o ( mem_req_o     ),
278	    .ready_i ( mem_gnt_i     )
279	  );
280	
281	  // Memory request output assignment
282	  for (genvar i = 0; unsigned'(i) < MemNumBanks; i++) begin : gen_mem_outp
283	    assign mem_add_o[i]   = mem_payload[i].addr;
284	    assign mem_we_o[i]    = mem_payload[i].we;
285	    assign mem_wdata_o[i] = mem_payload[i].wdata;
286	    assign mem_be_o[i]    = mem_payload[i].wstrb;
287	    assign mem_atop_o[i]  = mem_payload[i].atop;
288	  end
289	
290	// pragma translate_off
291	`ifndef VERILATOR
292	  initial begin: p_assertions
293	    assert (AxiIdWidth   >= 32'd1) else $fatal(1, "AxiIdWidth must be at least 1!");
294	    assert (AxiAddrWidth >= 32'd1) else $fatal(1, "AxiAddrWidth must be at least 1!");
295	    assert (AxiDataWidth >= 32'd1) else $fatal(1, "AxiDataWidth must be at least 1!");
296	    assert (MemNumBanks  >= 32'd2 * AxiDataWidth / MemDataWidth) else
297	        $fatal(1, "MemNumBanks has to be >= 2 * AxiDataWidth / MemDataWidth");
298	    assert (MemLatency   >= 32'd1) else $fatal(1, "MemLatency has to be at least 1!");
299	    assert ($onehot(MemNumBanks))  else $fatal(1, "MemNumBanks has to be a power of 2.");
300	    assert (MemAddrWidth >= 32'd1) else $fatal(1, "MemAddrWidth must be at least 1!");
301	    assert (MemDataWidth >= 32'd1) else $fatal(1, "MemDataWidth must be at least 1!");
302	    assert (AxiDataWidth % MemDataWidth == 0) else
303	        $fatal(1, "MemDataWidth has to be a divisor of AxiDataWidth.");
304	  end
305	`endif
306	// pragma translate_on
307	endmodule
308	
309	`include "axi/typedef.svh"
310	`include "axi/assign.svh"
311	/// AXI4+ATOP interface wrapper for `axi_to_mem`
312	module axi_to_mem_banked_intf #(
313	  /// AXI4+ATOP ID width
314	  parameter int unsigned                  AXI_ID_WIDTH   = 32'd0,
315	  /// AXI4+ATOP address width
316	  parameter int unsigned                  AXI_ADDR_WIDTH = 32'd0,
317	  /// AXI4+ATOP data width
318	  parameter int unsigned                  AXI_DATA_WIDTH = 32'd0,
319	  /// AXI4+ATOP user width
320	  parameter int unsigned                  AXI_USER_WIDTH = 32'd0,
321	  /// Number of memory banks / macros
322	  /// Has to satisfy:
323	  /// - MemNumBanks >= 2 * AxiDataWidth / MemDataWidth
324	  /// - MemNumBanks is a power of 2.
325	  parameter int unsigned                  MEM_NUM_BANKS  = 32'd4,
326	  /// Address width of an individual memory bank.
327	  parameter int unsigned                  MEM_ADDR_WIDTH = 32'd11,
328	  /// Data width of the memory macros.
329	  /// Has to satisfy:
330	  /// - AxiDataWidth % MemDataWidth = 0
331	  parameter int unsigned                  MEM_DATA_WIDTH = 32'd32,
332	  /// Read latency of the connected memory in cycles
333	  parameter int unsigned                  MEM_LATENCY    = 32'd1,
334	  /// Hide write requests if the strb == '0
335	  parameter bit                           HIDE_STRB      = 1'b0,
336	  /// Depth of output fifo/fall_through_register. Increase for asymmetric backpressure (contention) on banks.
337	  parameter int unsigned                  OUT_FIFO_DEPTH = 32'd1,
338	  // DEPENDENT PARAMETERS, DO NOT OVERWRITE!
339	  parameter type mem_addr_t = logic [MEM_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0],
340	  parameter type mem_atop_t = logic [5:0],
341	  parameter type mem_data_t = logic [MEM_DATA_WIDTH-1:0],
342	  parameter type mem_strb_t = logic [MEM_DATA_WIDTH/8-1:0]
343	) (
344	  /// Clock
345	  input  logic                          clk_i,
346	  /// Asynchronous reset, active low
347	  input  logic                          rst_ni,
348	  /// Testmode enable
349	  input  logic                          test_i,
350	  /// AXI4+ATOP slave port
351	  AXI_BUS.Slave                         slv,
352	  /// Memory bank request
353	  output logic      [MEM_NUM_BANKS-1:0] mem_req_o,
354	  /// Memory request grant
355	  input  logic      [MEM_NUM_BANKS-1:0] mem_gnt_i,
356	  /// Request address
357	  output mem_addr_t [MEM_NUM_BANKS-1:0] mem_add_o,
358	  /// Write request enable, active high
359	  output logic      [MEM_NUM_BANKS-1:0] mem_we_o,
360	  /// Write data
361	  output mem_data_t [MEM_NUM_BANKS-1:0] mem_wdata_o,
362	  /// Write data byte enable, active high
363	  output mem_strb_t [MEM_NUM_BANKS-1:0] mem_be_o,
364	  /// Atomic operation
365	  output mem_atop_t [MEM_NUM_BANKS-1:0] mem_atop_o,
366	  /// Read data response
367	  input  mem_data_t [MEM_NUM_BANKS-1:0] mem_rdata_i,
368	  /// Status output, busy flag of `axi_to_mem`
369	  output logic      [1:0]               axi_to_mem_busy_o
370	);
371	  typedef logic [AXI_ID_WIDTH-1:0]     id_t;
372	  typedef logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]   addr_t;
373	  typedef logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH-1:0]   data_t;
374	  typedef logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH/8-1:0] strb_t;
375	  typedef logic [AXI_USER_WIDTH-1:0]   user_t;
376	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_AW_CHAN_T(aw_chan_t, addr_t, id_t, user_t)
377	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_W_CHAN_T(w_chan_t, data_t, strb_t, user_t)
378	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_B_CHAN_T(b_chan_t, id_t, user_t)
379	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_AR_CHAN_T(ar_chan_t, addr_t, id_t, user_t)
380	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_R_CHAN_T(r_chan_t, data_t, id_t, user_t)
381	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_REQ_T(axi_req_t, aw_chan_t, w_chan_t, ar_chan_t)
382	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_RESP_T(axi_resp_t, b_chan_t, r_chan_t)
383	
384	  axi_req_t  mem_axi_req;
385	  axi_resp_t mem_axi_resp;
386	
387	  `AXI_ASSIGN_TO_REQ(mem_axi_req, slv)
388	  `AXI_ASSIGN_FROM_RESP(slv, mem_axi_resp)
389	
390	  axi_to_mem_banked #(
391	    .AxiIdWidth    ( AXI_ID_WIDTH               ),
392	    .AxiAddrWidth  ( AXI_ADDR_WIDTH             ),
393	    .AxiDataWidth  ( AXI_DATA_WIDTH             ),
394	    .axi_aw_chan_t ( aw_chan_t                  ),
395	    .axi_w_chan_t  (  w_chan_t                  ),
396	    .axi_b_chan_t  (  b_chan_t                  ),
397	    .axi_ar_chan_t ( ar_chan_t                  ),
398	    .axi_r_chan_t  (  r_chan_t                  ),
399	    .axi_req_t     ( axi_req_t                  ),
400	    .axi_resp_t    ( axi_resp_t                 ),
401	    .MemNumBanks   ( MEM_NUM_BANKS              ),
402	    .MemAddrWidth  ( MEM_ADDR_WIDTH             ),
403	    .MemDataWidth  ( MEM_DATA_WIDTH             ),
404	    .MemLatency    ( MEM_LATENCY                ),
405	    .HideStrb      ( HIDE_STRB                  ),
406	    .OutFifoDepth  ( OUT_FIFO_DEPTH             )
407	  ) i_axi_to_mem_banked (
408	    .clk_i,
409	    .rst_ni,
410	    .test_i,
411	    .axi_to_mem_busy_o,
412	    .axi_req_i      ( mem_axi_req  ),
413	    .axi_resp_o     ( mem_axi_resp ),
414	    .mem_req_o,
415	    .mem_gnt_i,
416	    .mem_add_o,
417	    .mem_wdata_o,
418	    .mem_be_o,
419	    .mem_atop_o,
420	    .mem_we_o,
421	    .mem_rdata_i
422	  );
423	
424	// pragma translate_off
425	`ifndef VERILATOR
426	  initial begin: p_assertions
427	    assert (AXI_ADDR_WIDTH  >= 1) else $fatal(1, "AXI address width must be at least 1!");
428	    assert (AXI_DATA_WIDTH  >= 1) else $fatal(1, "AXI data width must be at least 1!");
429	    assert (AXI_ID_WIDTH    >= 1) else $fatal(1, "AXI ID   width must be at least 1!");
430	    assert (AXI_USER_WIDTH  >= 1) else $fatal(1, "AXI user width must be at least 1!");
431	  end
432	`endif
433	// pragma translate_on
434	endmodule

16	/// AXI4+ATOP to SRAM memory slave. Allows for parallel read and write transactions.
17	/// Allows reads to bypass writes, in contrast to `axi_to_mem`, however needs more hardware.
18	module axi_to_mem_interleaved #(
19	  /// AXI4+ATOP request type. See `include/axi/typedef.svh`.
20	  parameter type         axi_req_t  = logic,
21	  /// AXI4+ATOP response type. See `include/axi/typedef.svh`.
22	  parameter type         axi_resp_t = logic,
23	  /// Address width, has to be less or equal than the width off the AXI address field.
24	  /// Determines the width of `mem_addr_o`. Has to be wide enough to emit the memory region
25	  /// which should be accessible.
26	  parameter int unsigned AddrWidth  = 0,
27	  /// AXI4+ATOP data width.
28	  parameter int unsigned DataWidth  = 0,
29	  /// AXI4+ATOP ID width.
30	  parameter int unsigned IdWidth    = 0,
31	  /// Number of banks at output, must evenly divide `DataWidth`.
32	  parameter int unsigned NumBanks   = 0,
33	  /// Depth of memory response buffer. This should be equal to the memory response latency.
34	  parameter int unsigned BufDepth   = 1,
35	  /// Hide write requests if the strb == '0
36	  parameter bit          HideStrb   = 1'b0,
37	  /// Depth of output fifo/fall_through_register. Increase for asymmetric backpressure (contention) on banks.
38	  parameter int unsigned OutFifoDepth = 1,
39	  /// Dependent parameter, do not override. Memory address type.
40	  parameter type addr_t     = logic [AddrWidth-1:0],
41	  /// Dependent parameter, do not override. Memory data type.
42	  parameter type mem_data_t = logic [DataWidth/NumBanks-1:0],
43	  /// Dependent parameter, do not override. Memory write strobe type.
44	  parameter type mem_strb_t = logic [DataWidth/NumBanks/8-1:0]
45	) (
46	  /// Clock input.
47	  input  logic                           clk_i,
48	  /// Asynchronous reset, active low.
49	  input  logic                           rst_ni,
50	  /// Testmode enable
51	  input  logic                           test_i,
52	  /// The unit is busy handling an AXI4+ATOP request.
53	  output logic                           busy_o,
54	  /// AXI4+ATOP slave port, request input.
55	  input  axi_req_t                       axi_req_i,
56	  /// AXI4+ATOP slave port, response output.
57	  output axi_resp_t                      axi_resp_o,
58	  /// Memory stream master, request is valid for this bank.
59	  output logic           [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_req_o,
60	  /// Memory stream master, request can be granted by this bank.
61	  input  logic           [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_gnt_i,
62	  /// Memory stream master, byte address of the request.
63	  output addr_t          [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_addr_o,
64	  /// Memory stream master, write data for this bank. Valid when `mem_req_o`.
65	  output mem_data_t      [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_wdata_o,
66	  /// Memory stream master, byte-wise strobe (byte enable).
67	  output mem_strb_t      [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_strb_o,
68	  /// Memory stream master, `axi_pkg::atop_t` signal associated with this request.
69	  output axi_pkg::atop_t [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_atop_o,
70	  /// Memory stream master, write enable. Then asserted store of `mem_w_data` is requested.
71	  output logic           [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_we_o,
72	  /// Memory stream master, response is valid. This module expects always a response valid for a
73	  /// request regardless if the request was a write or a read.
74	  input  logic           [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_rvalid_i,
75	  /// Memory stream master, read response data.
76	  input  mem_data_t      [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_rdata_i
77	);
78	
79	  // internal signals
80	  logic w_busy, r_busy;
81	  logic [NumBanks-1:0] arb_outcome, arb_outcome_head;
82	  logic [NumBanks-1:0] fifo_full;
83	  logic [NumBanks-1:0] r_axi_to_mem_req, w_axi_to_mem_req;
84	  logic [NumBanks-1:0] r_axi_to_mem_gnt, w_axi_to_mem_gnt;
85	
86	  // internal AXI buses
87	  axi_req_t  r_axi_req,  w_axi_req;
88	  axi_resp_t r_axi_resp, w_axi_resp;
89	
90	  // internal TCDM buses
91	  logic           [NumBanks-1:0]  r_mem_req,    w_mem_req;
92	  logic           [NumBanks-1:0]  r_mem_gnt,    w_mem_gnt;
93	  addr_t          [NumBanks-1:0]  r_mem_addr,   w_mem_addr;
94	  mem_data_t      [NumBanks-1:0]  r_mem_wdata,  w_mem_wdata;
95	  mem_strb_t      [NumBanks-1:0]  r_mem_strb,   w_mem_strb;
96	  axi_pkg::atop_t [NumBanks-1:0]  r_mem_atop,   w_mem_atop;
97	  logic           [NumBanks-1:0]  r_mem_we,     w_mem_we;
98	  logic           [NumBanks-1:0]  r_mem_rvalid, w_mem_rvalid;
99	  mem_data_t      [NumBanks-1:0]  r_mem_rdata,  w_mem_rdata;
100	
101	  assign busy_o = w_busy | r_busy;
102	
103	  // split AXI bus in read and write
104	  axi_demux_simple #(
105	    .AxiIdWidth  ( IdWidth    ),
106	    .AtopSupport ( 1'b1       ),
107	    .axi_req_t   ( axi_req_t  ),
108	    .axi_resp_t  ( axi_resp_t ),
109	    .NoMstPorts  ( 2          ),
110	    .MaxTrans    ( BufDepth   ),
111	    .AxiLookBits ( 1          ), // select is fixed, do not need it
112	    .UniqueIds   ( 1'b1       )  // Can be set as ports are statically selected -> reduces HW
113	  ) i_split_read_write (
114	    .clk_i,
115	    .rst_ni,
116	    .test_i,
117	    .slv_req_i       ( axi_req_i                ),
118	    .slv_ar_select_i ( 1'b0                     ),
119	    .slv_aw_select_i ( 1'b1                     ),
120	    .slv_resp_o      ( axi_resp_o               ),
121	    .mst_reqs_o      ( {w_axi_req,  r_axi_req}  ),
122	    .mst_resps_i     ( {w_axi_resp, r_axi_resp} )
123	  );
124	
125	  axi_to_mem #(
126	    .axi_req_t   ( axi_req_t    ),
127	    .axi_resp_t  ( axi_resp_t   ),
128	    .AddrWidth   ( AddrWidth    ),
129	    .DataWidth   ( DataWidth    ),
130	    .IdWidth     ( IdWidth      ),
131	    .NumBanks    ( NumBanks     ),
132	    .BufDepth    ( BufDepth     ),
133	    .HideStrb    ( HideStrb     ),
134	    .OutFifoDepth( OutFifoDepth )
135	  ) i_axi_to_mem_write (
136	    .clk_i        ( clk_i            ),
137	    .rst_ni       ( rst_ni           ),
138	    .busy_o       ( w_busy           ),
139	    .axi_req_i    ( w_axi_req        ),
140	    .axi_resp_o   ( w_axi_resp       ),
141	    .mem_req_o    ( w_axi_to_mem_req ),
142	    .mem_gnt_i    ( w_axi_to_mem_gnt ),
143	    .mem_addr_o   ( w_mem_addr       ),
144	    .mem_wdata_o  ( w_mem_wdata      ),
145	    .mem_strb_o   ( w_mem_strb       ),
146	    .mem_atop_o   ( w_mem_atop       ),
147	    .mem_we_o     ( w_mem_we         ),
148	    .mem_rvalid_i ( w_mem_rvalid     ),
149	    .mem_rdata_i  ( w_mem_rdata      )
150	  );
151	
152	  axi_to_mem #(
153	    .axi_req_t    ( axi_req_t    ),
154	    .axi_resp_t   ( axi_resp_t   ),
155	    .AddrWidth    ( AddrWidth    ),
156	    .DataWidth    ( DataWidth    ),
157	    .IdWidth      ( IdWidth      ),
158	    .NumBanks     ( NumBanks     ),
159	    .BufDepth     ( BufDepth     ),
160	    .HideStrb     ( HideStrb     ),
161	    .OutFifoDepth ( OutFifoDepth )
162	  ) i_axi_to_mem_read (
163	    .clk_i        ( clk_i            ),
164	    .rst_ni       ( rst_ni           ),
165	    .busy_o       ( r_busy           ),
166	    .axi_req_i    ( r_axi_req        ),
167	    .axi_resp_o   ( r_axi_resp       ),
168	    .mem_req_o    ( r_axi_to_mem_req ),
169	    .mem_gnt_i    ( r_axi_to_mem_gnt ),
170	    .mem_addr_o   ( r_mem_addr       ),
171	    .mem_wdata_o  ( r_mem_wdata      ),
172	    .mem_strb_o   ( r_mem_strb       ),
173	    .mem_atop_o   ( r_mem_atop       ),
174	    .mem_we_o     ( r_mem_we         ),
175	    .mem_rvalid_i ( r_mem_rvalid     ),
176	    .mem_rdata_i  ( r_mem_rdata      )
177	  );
178	
179	  // create a struct for the rr-arb-tree
180	  typedef struct packed {
181	    addr_t          addr;
182	    mem_data_t      wdata;
183	    mem_strb_t      strb;
184	    logic           we;
185	    axi_pkg::atop_t atop;
186	  } mem_req_payload_t;
187	
188	  mem_req_payload_t [NumBanks-1:0] r_payload, w_payload, payload;
189	
190	  for (genvar i = 0; i < NumBanks; i++) begin
191	    // pack the mem
192	    assign r_payload[i].addr  = r_mem_addr[i];
193	    assign r_payload[i].wdata = r_mem_wdata[i];
194	    assign r_payload[i].strb  = r_mem_strb[i];
195	    assign r_payload[i].we    = r_mem_we[i];
196	    assign r_payload[i].atop  = r_mem_atop[i];
197	
198	    assign w_payload[i].addr  = w_mem_addr[i];
199	    assign w_payload[i].wdata = w_mem_wdata[i];
200	    assign w_payload[i].strb  = w_mem_strb[i];
201	    assign w_payload[i].we    = w_mem_we[i];
202	    assign w_payload[i].atop  = w_mem_atop[i];
203	
204	    assign mem_addr_o [i] = payload[i].addr;
205	    assign mem_wdata_o[i] = payload[i].wdata;
206	    assign mem_strb_o [i] = payload[i].strb;
207	    assign mem_we_o   [i] = payload[i].we;
208	    assign mem_atop_o [i] = payload[i].atop;
209	
210	    // route data back to both channels
211	    assign w_mem_rdata[i]  = mem_rdata_i[i];
212	    assign r_mem_rdata[i]  = mem_rdata_i[i];
213	
214	    assign w_mem_rvalid[i] = mem_rvalid_i[i] & !arb_outcome_head[i];
215	    assign r_mem_rvalid[i] = mem_rvalid_i[i] &  arb_outcome_head[i];
216	
217	    assign w_mem_req[i] = w_axi_to_mem_req[i] & ~fifo_full[i];
218	    assign r_mem_req[i] = r_axi_to_mem_req[i] & ~fifo_full[i];
219	
220	    assign w_axi_to_mem_gnt[i] = w_mem_gnt[i] & ~fifo_full[i];
221	    assign r_axi_to_mem_gnt[i] = r_mem_gnt[i] & ~fifo_full[i];
222	
223	    // fine-grain arbitration
224	    rr_arb_tree #(
225	      .NumIn     ( 2                 ),
226	      .DataType  ( mem_req_payload_t )
227	    ) i_rr_arb_tree (
228	      .clk_i    ( clk_i                          ),
229	      .rst_ni   ( rst_ni                         ),
230	      .flush_i  ( 1'b0                           ),
231	      .rr_i     (  '0                            ),
232	      .req_i    ( { r_mem_req[i], w_mem_req[i] } ),
233	      .gnt_o    ( { r_mem_gnt[i], w_mem_gnt[i] } ),
234	      .data_i   ( { r_payload[i], w_payload[i] } ),
235	      .req_o    ( mem_req_o[i]                   ),
236	      .gnt_i    ( mem_gnt_i[i]                   ),
237	      .data_o   ( payload[i]                     ),
238	      .idx_o    ( arb_outcome[i]                 )
239	    );
240	
241	    // back-routing store
242	    fifo_v3 #(
243	      .DATA_WIDTH ( 1            ),
244	      .DEPTH      ( BufDepth + 1 )
245	    ) i_fifo_v3_response_trgt_store (
246	      .clk_i      ( clk_i                       ),
247	      .rst_ni     ( rst_ni                      ),
248	      .flush_i    ( 1'b0                        ),
249	      .testmode_i ( 1'b0                        ),
250	      .full_o     ( fifo_full[i]                ),
251	      .empty_o    ( ),
252	      .usage_o    ( ),
253	      .data_i     ( arb_outcome[i]              ),
254	      .push_i     ( mem_req_o[i] & mem_gnt_i[i] ),
255	      .data_o     ( arb_outcome_head[i]         ),
256	      .pop_i      ( mem_rvalid_i[i]             )
257	    );
258	  end
259	
260	endmodule : axi_to_mem_interleaved
261	
262	`include "axi/typedef.svh"
263	`include "axi/assign.svh"
264	/// AXI4+ATOP interface wrapper for `axi_to_mem_interleaved`
265	module axi_to_mem_interleaved_intf #(
266	  /// AXI4+ATOP ID width
267	  parameter int unsigned                  AXI_ID_WIDTH   = 32'd0,
268	  /// AXI4+ATOP address width
269	  parameter int unsigned                  AXI_ADDR_WIDTH = 32'd0,
270	  /// AXI4+ATOP data width
271	  parameter int unsigned                  AXI_DATA_WIDTH = 32'd0,
272	  /// AXI4+ATOP user width
273	  parameter int unsigned                  AXI_USER_WIDTH = 32'd0,
274	  /// Number of memory banks / macros
275	  parameter int unsigned                  MEM_NUM_BANKS  = 32'd4,
276	  /// Read latency of the connected memory in cycles
277	  parameter int unsigned                  BUF_DEPTH      = 32'd1,
278	  /// Hide write requests if the strb == '0
279	  parameter bit                           HIDE_STRB      = 1'b0,
280	  /// Depth of output fifo/fall_through_register. Increase for asymmetric backpressure (contention) on banks.
281	  parameter int unsigned                  OUT_FIFO_DEPTH = 32'd1,
282	
283	  /// Dependent parameter, do not override. Memory address type.
284	  parameter type mem_addr_t = logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0],
285	  /// Dependent parameter, do not override. Memory atomic type.
286	  parameter type mem_atop_t = axi_pkg::atop_t,
287	  /// Dependent parameter, do not override. Memory data type.
288	  parameter type mem_data_t = logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH/MEM_NUM_BANKS-1:0],
289	  /// Dependent parameter, do not override. Memory write strobe type.
290	  parameter type mem_strb_t = logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH/MEM_NUM_BANKS/8-1:0]
291	) (
292	  /// Clock
293	  input  logic                          clk_i,
294	  /// Asynchronous reset, active low
295	  input  logic                          rst_ni,
296	  /// Testmode enable
297	  input  logic                          test_i,
298	  /// Status output, busy flag of `axi_to_mem`
299	  output logic                          busy_o,
300	  /// AXI4+ATOP slave port
301	  AXI_BUS.Slave                         slv,
302	  /// Memory bank request
303	  output logic      [MEM_NUM_BANKS-1:0] mem_req_o,
304	  /// Memory request grant
305	  input  logic      [MEM_NUM_BANKS-1:0] mem_gnt_i,
306	  /// Request address
307	  output mem_addr_t [MEM_NUM_BANKS-1:0] mem_addr_o,
308	  /// Write data
309	  output mem_data_t [MEM_NUM_BANKS-1:0] mem_wdata_o,
310	  /// Write data byte enable, active high
311	  output mem_strb_t [MEM_NUM_BANKS-1:0] mem_strb_o,
312	  /// Atomic operation
313	  output mem_atop_t [MEM_NUM_BANKS-1:0] mem_atop_o,
314	  /// Write request enable, active high
315	  output logic      [MEM_NUM_BANKS-1:0] mem_we_o,
316	  /// Read data valid response, active high
317	  input  logic      [MEM_NUM_BANKS-1:0] mem_rvalid_i,
318	  /// Read data response
319	  input  mem_data_t [MEM_NUM_BANKS-1:0] mem_rdata_i
320	);
321	  typedef logic [AXI_ID_WIDTH-1:0]     id_t;
322	  typedef logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]   addr_t;
323	  typedef logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH-1:0]   data_t;
324	  typedef logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH/8-1:0] strb_t;
325	  typedef logic [AXI_USER_WIDTH-1:0]   user_t;
326	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_AW_CHAN_T(aw_chan_t, addr_t, id_t, user_t)
327	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_W_CHAN_T(w_chan_t, data_t, strb_t, user_t)
328	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_B_CHAN_T(b_chan_t, id_t, user_t)
329	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_AR_CHAN_T(ar_chan_t, addr_t, id_t, user_t)
330	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_R_CHAN_T(r_chan_t, data_t, id_t, user_t)
331	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_REQ_T(axi_req_t, aw_chan_t, w_chan_t, ar_chan_t)
332	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_RESP_T(axi_resp_t, b_chan_t, r_chan_t)
333	
334	  axi_req_t  mem_axi_req;
335	  axi_resp_t mem_axi_resp;
336	
337	  `AXI_ASSIGN_TO_REQ(mem_axi_req, slv)
338	  `AXI_ASSIGN_FROM_RESP(slv, mem_axi_resp)
339	
340	  axi_to_mem_interleaved #(
341	    .axi_req_t     ( axi_req_t      ),
342	    .axi_resp_t    ( axi_resp_t     ),
343	    .AddrWidth     ( AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ),
344	    .DataWidth     ( AXI_DATA_WIDTH ),
345	    .IdWidth       ( AXI_ID_WIDTH   ),
346	    .NumBanks      ( MEM_NUM_BANKS  ),
347	    .BufDepth      ( BUF_DEPTH      ),
348	    .HideStrb      ( HIDE_STRB      ),
349	    .OutFifoDepth  ( OUT_FIFO_DEPTH )
350	  ) i_axi_to_mem_interleaved (
351	    .clk_i,
352	    .rst_ni,
353	    .test_i,
354	    .busy_o,
355	    .axi_req_i  ( mem_axi_req  ),
356	    .axi_resp_o ( mem_axi_resp ),
357	    .mem_req_o,
358	    .mem_gnt_i,
359	    .mem_addr_o,
360	    .mem_wdata_o,
361	    .mem_strb_o,
362	    .mem_atop_o,
363	    .mem_we_o,
364	    .mem_rvalid_i,
365	    .mem_rdata_i
366	  );
367	
368	// pragma translate_off
369	`ifndef VERILATOR
370	  initial begin: p_assertions
371	    assert (AXI_ADDR_WIDTH  >= 1) else $fatal(1, "AXI address width must be at least 1!");
372	    assert (AXI_DATA_WIDTH  >= 1) else $fatal(1, "AXI data width must be at least 1!");
373	    assert (AXI_ID_WIDTH    >= 1) else $fatal(1, "AXI ID   width must be at least 1!");
374	    assert (AXI_USER_WIDTH  >= 1) else $fatal(1, "AXI user width must be at least 1!");
375	  end
376	`endif
377	// pragma translate_on
378	
379	endmodule // axi_to_mem_interleaved_intf

16	/// AXI4+ATOP to memory-protocol interconnect. Completely separates the read and write channel to
17	/// individual mem ports. This can only be used when addresses for the same bank are accessible
18	/// from different memory ports.
19	module axi_to_mem_split #(
20	  /// AXI4+ATOP request type. See `include/axi/typedef.svh`.
21	  parameter type         axi_req_t    = logic,
22	  /// AXI4+ATOP response type. See `include/axi/typedef.svh`.
23	  parameter type         axi_resp_t   = logic,
24	  /// Address width, has to be less or equal than the width off the AXI address field.
25	  /// Determines the width of `mem_addr_o`. Has to be wide enough to emit the memory region
26	  /// which should be accessible.
27	  parameter int unsigned AddrWidth    = 0,
28	  /// AXI4+ATOP data width.
29	  parameter int unsigned AxiDataWidth = 0,
30	  /// AXI4+ATOP ID width.
31	  parameter int unsigned IdWidth      = 0,
32	  /// Memory data width, must evenly divide `DataWidth`.
33	  parameter int unsigned MemDataWidth = 0, // must divide `AxiDataWidth` without remainder
34	  /// Depth of memory response buffer. This should be equal to the memory response latency.
35	  parameter int unsigned BufDepth     = 0,
36	  /// Hide write requests if the strb == '0
37	  parameter bit          HideStrb   = 1'b0,
38	  /// Depth of output fifo/fall_through_register. Increase for asymmetric backpressure (contention) on banks.
39	  parameter int unsigned OutFifoDepth = 1,
40	  /// Dependent parameters, do not override. Number of memory ports.
41	  parameter int unsigned NumMemPorts  = 2*AxiDataWidth/MemDataWidth,
42	  /// Dependent parameter, do not override. Memory address type.
43	  parameter type         addr_t       = logic [AddrWidth-1:0],
44	  /// Dependent parameter, do not override. Memory data type.
45	  parameter type         mem_data_t   = logic [MemDataWidth-1:0],
46	  /// Dependent parameter, do not override. Memory write strobe type.
47	  parameter type         mem_strb_t   = logic [MemDataWidth/8-1:0]
48	) (
49	  /// Clock input.
50	  input logic                              clk_i,
51	  /// Asynchronous reset, active low.
52	  input logic                              rst_ni,
53	  /// Testmode enable
54	  input  logic                             test_i,
55	  /// The unit is busy handling an AXI4+ATOP request.
56	  output logic                             busy_o,
57	  /// AXI4+ATOP slave port, request input.
58	  input  axi_req_t                         axi_req_i,
59	  /// AXI4+ATOP slave port, response output.
60	  output axi_resp_t                        axi_resp_o,
61	  /// Memory stream master, request is valid for this bank.
62	  output logic           [NumMemPorts-1:0] mem_req_o,
63	  /// Memory stream master, request can be granted by this bank.
64	  input  logic           [NumMemPorts-1:0] mem_gnt_i,
65	  /// Memory stream master, byte address of the request.
66	  output addr_t          [NumMemPorts-1:0] mem_addr_o,   // byte address
67	  /// Memory stream master, write data for this bank. Valid when `mem_req_o`.
68	  output mem_data_t      [NumMemPorts-1:0] mem_wdata_o,  // write data
69	  /// Memory stream master, byte-wise strobe (byte enable).
70	  output mem_strb_t      [NumMemPorts-1:0] mem_strb_o,   // byte-wise strobe
71	  /// Memory stream master, `axi_pkg::atop_t` signal associated with this request.
72	  output axi_pkg::atop_t [NumMemPorts-1:0] mem_atop_o,   // atomic operation
73	  /// Memory stream master, write enable. Then asserted store of `mem_w_data` is requested.
74	  output logic           [NumMemPorts-1:0] mem_we_o,     // write enable
75	  /// Memory stream master, response is valid. This module expects always a response valid for a
76	  /// request regardless if the request was a write or a read.
77	  input  logic           [NumMemPorts-1:0] mem_rvalid_i, // response valid
78	  /// Memory stream master, read response data.
79	  input  mem_data_t      [NumMemPorts-1:0] mem_rdata_i   // read data
80	);
81	
82	  axi_req_t axi_read_req, axi_write_req;
83	  axi_resp_t axi_read_resp, axi_write_resp;
84	
85	  logic read_busy, write_busy;
86	
87	  // split AXI bus in read and write
88	  axi_demux_simple #(
89	    .AxiIdWidth  ( IdWidth    ),
90	    .AtopSupport ( 1'b1       ),
91	    .axi_req_t   ( axi_req_t  ),
92	    .axi_resp_t  ( axi_resp_t ),
93	    .NoMstPorts  ( 2          ),
94	    .MaxTrans    ( BufDepth   ),
95	    .AxiLookBits ( 1          ), // select is fixed, do not need it
96	    .UniqueIds   ( 1'b1       )  // Can be set as ports are statically selected -> reduces HW
97	  ) i_split_read_write (
98	    .clk_i,
99	    .rst_ni,
100	    .test_i,
101	    .slv_req_i       ( axi_req_i                       ),
102	    .slv_ar_select_i ( 1'b0                            ),
103	    .slv_aw_select_i ( 1'b1                            ),
104	    .slv_resp_o      ( axi_resp_o                      ),
105	    .mst_reqs_o      ( {axi_write_req,  axi_read_req}  ),
106	    .mst_resps_i     ( {axi_write_resp, axi_read_resp} )
107	  );
108	
109	  assign busy_o = read_busy || write_busy;
110	
111	  axi_to_mem #(
112	    .axi_req_t    ( axi_req_t     ),
113	    .axi_resp_t   ( axi_resp_t    ),
114	    .AddrWidth    ( AddrWidth     ),
115	    .DataWidth    ( AxiDataWidth  ),
116	    .IdWidth      ( IdWidth       ),
117	    .NumBanks     ( NumMemPorts/2 ),
118	    .BufDepth     ( BufDepth      ),
119	    .HideStrb     ( 1'b0          ),
120	    .OutFifoDepth ( OutFifoDepth  )
121	  ) i_axi_to_mem_read (
122	    .clk_i,
123	    .rst_ni,
124	    .busy_o       ( read_busy                        ),
125	    .axi_req_i    ( axi_read_req                     ),
126	    .axi_resp_o   ( axi_read_resp                    ),
127	    .mem_req_o    ( mem_req_o    [NumMemPorts/2-1:0] ),
128	    .mem_gnt_i    ( mem_gnt_i    [NumMemPorts/2-1:0] ),
129	    .mem_addr_o   ( mem_addr_o   [NumMemPorts/2-1:0] ),
130	    .mem_wdata_o  ( mem_wdata_o  [NumMemPorts/2-1:0] ),
131	    .mem_strb_o   ( mem_strb_o   [NumMemPorts/2-1:0] ),
132	    .mem_atop_o   ( mem_atop_o   [NumMemPorts/2-1:0] ),
133	    .mem_we_o     ( mem_we_o     [NumMemPorts/2-1:0] ),
134	    .mem_rvalid_i ( mem_rvalid_i [NumMemPorts/2-1:0] ),
135	    .mem_rdata_i  ( mem_rdata_i  [NumMemPorts/2-1:0] )
136	  );
137	
138	  axi_to_mem #(
139	    .axi_req_t    ( axi_req_t     ),
140	    .axi_resp_t   ( axi_resp_t    ),
141	    .AddrWidth    ( AddrWidth     ),
142	    .DataWidth    ( AxiDataWidth  ),
143	    .IdWidth      ( IdWidth       ),
144	    .NumBanks     ( NumMemPorts/2 ),
145	    .BufDepth     ( BufDepth      ),
146	    .HideStrb     ( HideStrb      ),
147	    .OutFifoDepth ( OutFifoDepth  )
148	  ) i_axi_to_mem_write (
149	    .clk_i,
150	    .rst_ni,
151	    .busy_o       ( write_busy                                 ),
152	    .axi_req_i    ( axi_write_req                              ),
153	    .axi_resp_o   ( axi_write_resp                             ),
154	    .mem_req_o    ( mem_req_o    [NumMemPorts-1:NumMemPorts/2] ),
155	    .mem_gnt_i    ( mem_gnt_i    [NumMemPorts-1:NumMemPorts/2] ),
156	    .mem_addr_o   ( mem_addr_o   [NumMemPorts-1:NumMemPorts/2] ),
157	    .mem_wdata_o  ( mem_wdata_o  [NumMemPorts-1:NumMemPorts/2] ),
158	    .mem_strb_o   ( mem_strb_o   [NumMemPorts-1:NumMemPorts/2] ),
159	    .mem_atop_o   ( mem_atop_o   [NumMemPorts-1:NumMemPorts/2] ),
160	    .mem_we_o     ( mem_we_o     [NumMemPorts-1:NumMemPorts/2] ),
161	    .mem_rvalid_i ( mem_rvalid_i [NumMemPorts-1:NumMemPorts/2] ),
162	    .mem_rdata_i  ( mem_rdata_i  [NumMemPorts-1:NumMemPorts/2] )
163	  );
164	
165	endmodule
166	
167	`include "axi/typedef.svh"
168	/// AXI4+ATOP interface wrapper for `axi_to_mem_split`
169	module axi_to_mem_split_intf #(
170	  /// AXI4+ATOP ID width
171	  parameter int unsigned AXI_ID_WIDTH   = 32'b0,
172	  /// AXI4+ATOP address width
173	  parameter int unsigned AXI_ADDR_WIDTH = 32'b0,
174	  /// AXI4+ATOP data width
175	  parameter int unsigned AXI_DATA_WIDTH = 32'b0,
176	  /// AXI4+ATOP user width
177	  parameter int unsigned AXI_USER_WIDTH = 32'b0,
178	  /// Memory data width, must evenly divide `DataWidth`.
179	  parameter int unsigned MEM_DATA_WIDTH = 32'b0,
180	  /// See `axi_to_mem`, parameter `BufDepth`.
181	  parameter int unsigned BUF_DEPTH      = 0,
182	  /// Hide write requests if the strb == '0
183	  parameter bit          HIDE_STRB      = 1'b0,
184	  /// Depth of output fifo/fall_through_register. Increase for asymmetric backpressure (contention) on banks.
185	  parameter int unsigned OUT_FIFO_DEPTH = 32'd1,
186	  /// Dependent parameters, do not override. Number of memory ports.
187	  parameter int unsigned NUM_MEM_PORTS  = 2*AXI_DATA_WIDTH/MEM_DATA_WIDTH,
188	  /// Dependent parameter, do not override. See `axi_to_mem`, parameter `addr_t`.
189	  parameter type addr_t     = logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0],
190	  /// Dependent parameter, do not override. See `axi_to_mem`, parameter `mem_data_t`.
191	  parameter type mem_data_t = logic [MEM_DATA_WIDTH-1:0],
192	  /// Dependent parameter, do not override. See `axi_to_mem`, parameter `mem_strb_t`.
193	  parameter type mem_strb_t = logic [MEM_DATA_WIDTH/8-1:0]
194	) (
195	  /// Clock input.
196	  input  logic                               clk_i,
197	  /// Asynchronous reset, active low.
198	  input  logic                               rst_ni,
199	  /// Testmode enable
200	  input  logic                               test_i,
201	  /// See `axi_to_mem_split`, port `busy_o`.
202	  output logic                               busy_o,
203	  /// AXI4+ATOP slave interface port.
204	  AXI_BUS.Slave                              axi_bus,
205	  /// See `axi_to_mem_split`, port `mem_req_o`.
206	  output logic           [NUM_MEM_PORTS-1:0] mem_req_o,
207	  /// See `axi_to_mem_split`, port `mem_gnt_i`.
208	  input  logic           [NUM_MEM_PORTS-1:0] mem_gnt_i,
209	  /// See `axi_to_mem_split`, port `mem_addr_o`.
210	  output addr_t          [NUM_MEM_PORTS-1:0] mem_addr_o,
211	  /// See `axi_to_mem_split`, port `mem_wdata_o`.
212	  output mem_data_t      [NUM_MEM_PORTS-1:0] mem_wdata_o,
213	  /// See `axi_to_mem_split`, port `mem_strb_o`.
214	  output mem_strb_t      [NUM_MEM_PORTS-1:0] mem_strb_o,
215	  /// See `axi_to_mem_split`, port `mem_atop_o`.
216	  output axi_pkg::atop_t [NUM_MEM_PORTS-1:0] mem_atop_o,
217	  /// See `axi_to_mem_split`, port `mem_we_o`.
218	  output logic           [NUM_MEM_PORTS-1:0] mem_we_o,
219	  /// See `axi_to_mem_split`, port `mem_rvalid_i`.
220	  input  logic           [NUM_MEM_PORTS-1:0] mem_rvalid_i,
221	  /// See `axi_to_mem_split`, port `mem_rdata_i`.
222	  input  mem_data_t      [NUM_MEM_PORTS-1:0] mem_rdata_i
223	);
224	
225	  typedef logic [AXI_ID_WIDTH-1:0] id_t;
226	  typedef logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH-1:0] data_t;
227	  typedef logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH/8-1:0] strb_t;
228	  typedef logic [AXI_USER_WIDTH-1:0] user_t;
229	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_ALL(axi, addr_t, id_t, data_t, strb_t, user_t)
230	
231	  axi_req_t axi_req;
232	  axi_resp_t axi_resp;
233	  `AXI_ASSIGN_TO_REQ(axi_req, axi_bus)
234	  `AXI_ASSIGN_FROM_RESP(axi_bus, axi_resp)
235	
236	  axi_to_mem_split #(
237	    .axi_req_t    ( axi_req_t      ),
238	    .axi_resp_t   ( axi_resp_t     ),
239	    .AxiDataWidth ( AXI_DATA_WIDTH ),
240	    .AddrWidth    ( AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ),
241	    .IdWidth      ( AXI_ID_WIDTH   ),
242	    .MemDataWidth ( MEM_DATA_WIDTH ), // must divide `AxiDataWidth` without remainder
243	    .BufDepth     ( BUF_DEPTH      ),
244	    .HideStrb     ( HIDE_STRB      ),
245	    .OutFifoDepth ( OUT_FIFO_DEPTH )
246	  ) i_axi_to_mem_split (
247	    .clk_i,
248	    .rst_ni,
249	    .test_i,
250	    .busy_o,
251	    .axi_req_i (axi_req),
252	    .axi_resp_o (axi_resp),
253	    .mem_req_o,
254	    .mem_gnt_i,
255	    .mem_addr_o,
256	    .mem_wdata_o,
257	    .mem_strb_o,
258	    .mem_atop_o,
259	    .mem_we_o,
260	    .mem_rvalid_i,
261	    .mem_rdata_i
262	  );
263	
264	endmodule

12	// - Michael Rogenmoser <michaero@iis.ee.ethz.ch>
13	// - Thomas Benz <tbenz@iis.ee.ethz.ch>
14	
15	`include "common_cells/registers.svh"
16	/// AXI4+ATOP slave module which translates AXI bursts into a memory stream.
17	/// If both read and write channels of the AXI4+ATOP are active, both will have an
18	/// utilization of 50%.
19	module axi_to_detailed_mem #(
20	  /// AXI4+ATOP request type. See `include/axi/typedef.svh`.
21	  parameter type         axi_req_t  = logic,
22	  /// AXI4+ATOP response type. See `include/axi/typedef.svh`.
23	  parameter type         axi_resp_t = logic,
24	  /// Address width, has to be less or equal than the width off the AXI address field.
25	  /// Determines the width of `mem_addr_o`. Has to be wide enough to emit the memory region
26	  /// which should be accessible.
27	  parameter int unsigned AddrWidth  = 1,
28	  /// AXI4+ATOP data width.
29	  parameter int unsigned DataWidth  = 1,
30	  /// AXI4+ATOP ID width.
31	  parameter int unsigned IdWidth    = 1,
32	  /// AXI4+ATOP user width.
33	  parameter int unsigned UserWidth  = 1,
34	  /// Number of banks at output, must evenly divide `DataWidth`.
35	  parameter int unsigned NumBanks   = 1,
36	  /// Depth of memory response buffer. This should be equal to the memory response latency.
37	  parameter int unsigned BufDepth   = 1,
38	  /// Hide write requests if the strb == '0
39	  parameter bit          HideStrb   = 1'b0,
40	  /// Depth of output fifo/fall_through_register. Increase for asymmetric backpressure (contention) on banks.
41	  parameter int unsigned OutFifoDepth = 1,
42	  /// Dependent parameter, do not override. Memory address type.
43	  localparam type addr_t     = logic [AddrWidth-1:0],
44	  /// Dependent parameter, do not override. Memory data type.
45	  localparam type mem_data_t = logic [DataWidth/NumBanks-1:0],
46	  /// Dependent parameter, do not override. Memory write strobe type.
47	  localparam type mem_strb_t = logic [DataWidth/NumBanks/8-1:0],
48	  /// Dependent parameter, do not override. Memory id type.
49	  localparam type mem_id_t   = logic [IdWidth-1:0],
50	  /// Dependent parameter, do not override. Memory user type.
51	  localparam type mem_user_t = logic [UserWidth-1:0]
52	) (
53	  /// Clock input.
54	  input  logic                             clk_i,
55	  /// Asynchronous reset, active low.
56	  input  logic                             rst_ni,
57	  /// The unit is busy handling an AXI4+ATOP request.
58	  output logic                             busy_o,
59	  /// AXI4+ATOP slave port, request input.
60	  input  axi_req_t                         axi_req_i,
61	  /// AXI4+ATOP slave port, response output.
62	  output axi_resp_t                        axi_resp_o,
63	  /// Memory stream master, request is valid for this bank.
64	  output logic             [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_req_o,
65	  /// Memory stream master, request can be granted by this bank.
66	  input  logic             [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_gnt_i,
67	  /// Memory stream master, byte address of the request.
68	  output addr_t            [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_addr_o,
69	  /// Memory stream master, write data for this bank. Valid when `mem_req_o`.
70	  output mem_data_t        [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_wdata_o,
71	  /// Memory stream master, byte-wise strobe (byte enable).
72	  output mem_strb_t        [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_strb_o,
73	  /// Memory stream master, `axi_pkg::atop_t` signal associated with this request.
74	  output axi_pkg::atop_t   [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_atop_o,
75	  /// Memory stream master, lock signal.
76	  output logic             [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_lock_o,
77	  /// Memory stream master, write enable. Then asserted store of `mem_w_data` is requested.
78	  output logic             [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_we_o,
79	  /// Memory stream master, ID. Response ID is managed internally, ensure in-order responses.
80	  output mem_id_t          [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_id_o,
81	  /// Memory stream master, user signal. Ax channel user bits used.
82	  output mem_user_t        [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_user_o,
83	  /// Memory stream master, cache signal.
84	  output axi_pkg::cache_t  [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_cache_o,
85	  /// Memory stream master, protection signal.
86	  output axi_pkg::prot_t   [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_prot_o,
87	  /// Memory stream master, QOS signal.
88	  output axi_pkg::qos_t    [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_qos_o,
89	  /// Memory stream master, region signal.
90	  output axi_pkg::region_t [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_region_o,
91	  /// Memory stream master, response is valid. This module expects always a response valid for a
92	  /// request regardless if the request was a write or a read.
93	  input  logic             [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_rvalid_i,
94	  /// Memory stream master, read response data.
95	  input  mem_data_t        [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_rdata_i,
96	  /// Memory stream master, error response.
97	  input  logic             [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_err_i,
98	  /// Memory stream master, read response exclusive access OK.
99	  input  logic             [NumBanks-1:0]  mem_exokay_i
100	);
101	
102	  typedef logic [DataWidth-1:0]   axi_data_t;
103	  typedef logic [DataWidth/8-1:0] axi_strb_t;
104	  typedef logic [IdWidth-1:0]     axi_id_t;
105	
106	  typedef struct packed {
107	    addr_t            addr;
108	    axi_pkg::atop_t   atop;
109	    logic             lock;
110	    axi_strb_t        strb;
111	    axi_data_t        wdata;
112	    logic             we;
113	    mem_id_t          id;
114	    mem_user_t        user;
115	    axi_pkg::cache_t  cache;
116	    axi_pkg::prot_t   prot;
117	    axi_pkg::qos_t    qos;
118	    axi_pkg::region_t region;
119	  } mem_req_t;
120	
121	  typedef struct packed {
122	    addr_t            addr;
123	    axi_pkg::atop_t   atop;
124	    logic             lock;
125	    axi_strb_t        strb;
126	    axi_id_t          id;
127	    logic             last;
128	    axi_pkg::qos_t    qos;
129	    axi_pkg::size_t   size;
130	    logic             write;
131	    mem_user_t        user;
132	    axi_pkg::cache_t  cache;
133	    axi_pkg::prot_t   prot;
134	    axi_pkg::region_t region;
135	  } meta_t;
136	
137	  typedef struct packed {
138	    axi_data_t           data;
139	    logic [NumBanks-1:0] err;
140	    logic [NumBanks-1:0] exokay;
141	  } mem_rsp_t;
142	
143	  mem_rsp_t       m2s_resp;
144	  axi_pkg::len_t  r_cnt_d,        r_cnt_q,
145	                  w_cnt_d,        w_cnt_q;
146	  logic           arb_valid,      arb_ready,
147	                  rd_valid,       rd_ready,
148	                  wr_valid,       wr_ready,
149	                  sel_b,          sel_buf_b,
150	                  sel_r,          sel_buf_r,
151	                  sel_valid,      sel_ready,
152	                  sel_buf_valid,  sel_buf_ready,
153	                  sel_lock_d,     sel_lock_q,
154	                  meta_valid,     meta_ready,
155	                  meta_buf_valid, meta_buf_ready,
156	                  meta_sel_d,     meta_sel_q,
157	                  m2s_req_valid,  m2s_req_ready,
158	                  m2s_resp_valid, m2s_resp_ready;
159	  mem_req_t       m2s_req;
160	  meta_t          rd_meta,
161	                  rd_meta_d,      rd_meta_q,
162	                  wr_meta,
163	                  wr_meta_d,      wr_meta_q,
164	                  meta,           meta_buf;
165	
166	  assign busy_o = axi_req_i.aw_valid | axi_req_i.ar_valid | axi_req_i.w_valid |
167	                    axi_resp_o.b_valid | axi_resp_o.r_valid |
168	                    (r_cnt_q > 0) | (w_cnt_q > 0);
169	
170	  // Handle reads.
171	  always_comb begin
172	    // Default assignments
173	    axi_resp_o.ar_ready = 1'b0;
174	    rd_meta_d           = rd_meta_q;
175	    rd_meta             = meta_t'{default: '0};
176	    rd_valid            = 1'b0;
177	    r_cnt_d             = r_cnt_q;
178	    // Handle R burst in progress.
179	    if (r_cnt_q > '0) begin
180	      rd_meta_d.last = (r_cnt_q == 8'd1);
181	      rd_meta        = rd_meta_d;
182	      rd_meta.addr   = rd_meta_q.addr + axi_pkg::num_bytes(rd_meta_q.size);
183	      rd_valid       = 1'b1;
184	      if (rd_ready) begin
185	        r_cnt_d--;
186	        rd_meta_d.addr = rd_meta.addr;
187	      end
188	    // Handle new AR if there is one.
189	    end else if (axi_req_i.ar_valid) begin
190	      rd_meta_d = '{
191	        addr:   addr_t'(axi_pkg::aligned_addr(axi_pkg::largest_addr_t'(axi_req_i.ar.addr), axi_req_i.ar.size)),
192	        atop:   '0,
193	        lock:   axi_req_i.ar.lock,
194	        strb:   '0,
195	        id:     axi_req_i.ar.id,
196	        last:   (axi_req_i.ar.len == '0),
197	        qos:    axi_req_i.ar.qos,
198	        size:   axi_req_i.ar.size,
199	        write:  1'b0,
200	        user:   axi_req_i.ar.user,
201	        cache:  axi_req_i.ar.cache,
202	        prot:   axi_req_i.ar.prot,
203	        region: axi_req_i.ar.region
204	      };
205	      rd_meta      = rd_meta_d;
206	      rd_meta.addr = addr_t'(axi_req_i.ar.addr);
207	      rd_valid     = 1'b1;
208	      if (rd_ready) begin
209	        r_cnt_d             = axi_req_i.ar.len;
210	        axi_resp_o.ar_ready = 1'b1;
211	      end
212	    end
213	  end
214	
215	  // Handle writes.
216	  always_comb begin
217	    // Default assignments
218	    axi_resp_o.aw_ready = 1'b0;
219	    axi_resp_o.w_ready  = 1'b0;
220	    wr_meta_d           = wr_meta_q;
221	    wr_meta             = meta_t'{default: '0};
222	    wr_valid            = 1'b0;
223	    w_cnt_d             = w_cnt_q;
224	    // Handle W bursts in progress.
225	    if (w_cnt_q > '0) begin
226	      wr_meta_d.last = (w_cnt_q == 8'd1);
227	      wr_meta        = wr_meta_d;
228	      wr_meta.addr   = wr_meta_q.addr + axi_pkg::num_bytes(wr_meta_q.size);
229	      if (axi_req_i.w_valid) begin
230	        wr_valid = 1'b1;
231	        wr_meta.strb = axi_req_i.w.strb;
232	        if (wr_ready) begin
233	          axi_resp_o.w_ready = 1'b1;
234	          w_cnt_d--;
235	          wr_meta_d.addr = wr_meta.addr;
236	        end
237	      end
238	    // Handle new AW if there is one.
239	    end else if (axi_req_i.aw_valid && axi_req_i.w_valid) begin
240	      wr_meta_d = '{
241	        addr:   addr_t'(axi_pkg::aligned_addr(axi_pkg::largest_addr_t'(axi_req_i.aw.addr), axi_req_i.aw.size)),
242	        atop:   axi_req_i.aw.atop,
243	        lock:   axi_req_i.aw.lock,
244	        strb:   axi_req_i.w.strb,
245	        id:     axi_req_i.aw.id,
246	        last:   (axi_req_i.aw.len == '0),
247	        qos:    axi_req_i.aw.qos,
248	        size:   axi_req_i.aw.size,
249	        write:  1'b1,
250	        user:   axi_req_i.aw.user,
251	        cache:  axi_req_i.aw.cache,
252	        prot:   axi_req_i.aw.prot,
253	        region: axi_req_i.aw.region
254	      };
255	      wr_meta = wr_meta_d;
256	      wr_meta.addr = addr_t'(axi_req_i.aw.addr);
257	      wr_valid = 1'b1;
258	      if (wr_ready) begin
259	        w_cnt_d = axi_req_i.aw.len;
260	        axi_resp_o.aw_ready = 1'b1;
261	        axi_resp_o.w_ready = 1'b1;
262	      end
263	    end
264	  end
265	
266	  // Arbitrate between reads and writes.
267	  stream_mux #(
268	    .DATA_T ( meta_t ),
269	    .N_INP  ( 32'd2  )
270	  ) i_ax_mux (
271	    .inp_data_i   ({wr_meta,  rd_meta }),
272	    .inp_valid_i  ({wr_valid, rd_valid}),
273	    .inp_ready_o  ({wr_ready, rd_ready}),
274	    .inp_sel_i    ( meta_sel_d         ),
275	    .oup_data_o   ( meta               ),
276	    .oup_valid_o  ( arb_valid          ),
277	    .oup_ready_i  ( arb_ready          )
278	  );
279	  always_comb begin
280	    meta_sel_d = meta_sel_q;
281	    sel_lock_d = sel_lock_q;
282	    if (sel_lock_q) begin
283	      meta_sel_d = meta_sel_q;
284	      if (arb_valid && arb_ready) begin
285	        sel_lock_d = 1'b0;
286	      end
287	    end else begin
288	      if (wr_valid ^ rd_valid) begin
289	        // If either write or read is valid but not both, select the valid one.
290	        meta_sel_d = wr_valid;
291	      end else if (wr_valid && rd_valid) begin
292	        // If both write and read are valid, decide according to QoS then burst properties.
293	        // Prioritize higher QoS.
294	        if (wr_meta.qos > rd_meta.qos) begin
295	          meta_sel_d = 1'b1;
296	        end else if (rd_meta.qos > wr_meta.qos) begin
297	          meta_sel_d = 1'b0;
298	        // Decide requests with identical QoS.
299	        end else if (wr_meta.qos == rd_meta.qos) begin
300	          // 1. Prioritize individual writes over read bursts.
301	          // Rationale: Read bursts can be interleaved on AXI but write bursts cannot.
302	          if (wr_meta.last && !rd_meta.last) begin
303	            meta_sel_d = 1'b1;
304	          // 2. Prioritize ongoing burst.
305	          // Rationale: Stalled bursts create back-pressure or require costly buffers.
306	          end else if (w_cnt_q > '0) begin
307	            meta_sel_d = 1'b1;
308	          end else if (r_cnt_q > '0) begin
309	            meta_sel_d = 1'b0;
310	          // 3. Otherwise arbitrate round robin to prevent starvation.
311	          end else begin
312	            meta_sel_d = ~meta_sel_q;
313	          end
314	        end
315	      end
316	      // Lock arbitration if valid but not yet ready.
317	      if (arb_valid && !arb_ready) begin
318	        sel_lock_d = 1'b1;
319	      end
320	    end
321	  end
322	
323	  // Fork arbitrated stream to meta data, memory requests, and R/B channel selection.
324	  stream_fork #(
325	    .N_OUP ( 32'd3 )
326	  ) i_fork (
327	    .clk_i,
328	    .rst_ni,
329	    .valid_i ( arb_valid                            ),
330	    .ready_o ( arb_ready                            ),
331	    .valid_o ({sel_valid, meta_valid, m2s_req_valid}),
332	    .ready_i ({sel_ready, meta_ready, m2s_req_ready})
333	  );
334	
335	  assign sel_b = meta.write & meta.last;
336	  assign sel_r = ~meta.write | meta.atop[5];
337	
338	  stream_fifo #(
339	    .FALL_THROUGH ( 1'b1             ),
340	    .DEPTH        ( 32'd1 + BufDepth ),
341	    .T            ( logic[1:0]       )
342	  ) i_sel_buf (
343	    .clk_i,
344	    .rst_ni,
345	    .flush_i    ( 1'b0                    ),
346	    .testmode_i ( 1'b0                    ),
347	    .data_i     ({sel_b,        sel_r    }),
348	    .valid_i    ( sel_valid               ),
349	    .ready_o    ( sel_ready               ),
350	    .data_o     ({sel_buf_b,    sel_buf_r}),
351	    .valid_o    ( sel_buf_valid           ),
352	    .ready_i    ( sel_buf_ready           ),
353	    .usage_o    ( /* unused */            )
354	  );
355	
356	  stream_fifo #(
357	    .FALL_THROUGH ( 1'b1             ),
358	    .DEPTH        ( 32'd1 + BufDepth ),
359	    .T            ( meta_t           )
360	  ) i_meta_buf (
361	    .clk_i,
362	    .rst_ni,
363	    .flush_i    ( 1'b0           ),
364	    .testmode_i ( 1'b0           ),
365	    .data_i     ( meta           ),
366	    .valid_i    ( meta_valid     ),
367	    .ready_o    ( meta_ready     ),
368	    .data_o     ( meta_buf       ),
369	    .valid_o    ( meta_buf_valid ),
370	    .ready_i    ( meta_buf_ready ),
371	    .usage_o    ( /* unused */   )
372	  );
373	
374	  // Assemble the actual memory request from meta information and write data.
375	  assign m2s_req = mem_req_t'{
376	    addr:   meta.addr,
377	    atop:   meta.atop,
378	    lock:   meta.lock,
379	    strb:   axi_req_i.w.strb,
380	    wdata:  axi_req_i.w.data,
381	    we:     meta.write,
382	    id:     meta.id,
383	    user:   meta.user,
384	    cache:  meta.cache,
385	    prot:   meta.prot,
386	    qos:    meta.qos,
387	    region: meta.region
388	  };
389	
390	  typedef struct packed {
391	    axi_pkg::atop_t   atop;
392	    logic             lock;
393	    mem_id_t          id;
394	    mem_user_t        user;
395	    axi_pkg::cache_t  cache;
396	    axi_pkg::prot_t   prot;
397	    axi_pkg::qos_t    qos;
398	    axi_pkg::region_t region;
399	  } tmp_atop_t;
400	
401	  tmp_atop_t mem_req_atop;
402	  tmp_atop_t [NumBanks-1:0] banked_req_atop;
403	
404	  assign mem_req_atop = '{
405	    atop:   m2s_req.atop,
406	    lock:   m2s_req.lock,
407	    id:     m2s_req.id,
408	    user:   m2s_req.user,
409	    cache:  m2s_req.cache,
410	    prot:   m2s_req.prot,
411	    qos:    m2s_req.qos,
412	    region: m2s_req.region
413	  };
414	
415	  for (genvar i = 0; i < NumBanks; i++) begin
416	    assign mem_atop_o  [i] = banked_req_atop[i].atop;
417	    assign mem_lock_o  [i] = banked_req_atop[i].lock;
418	    assign mem_id_o    [i] = banked_req_atop[i].id;
419	    assign mem_user_o  [i] = banked_req_atop[i].user;
420	    assign mem_cache_o [i] = banked_req_atop[i].cache;
421	    assign mem_prot_o  [i] = banked_req_atop[i].prot;
422	    assign mem_qos_o   [i] = banked_req_atop[i].qos;
423	    assign mem_region_o[i] = banked_req_atop[i].region;
424	  end
425	
426	  logic [NumBanks-1:0][1:0] tmp_ersp;
427	  logic [NumBanks-1:0][1:0] bank_ersp;
428	  for (genvar i = 0; i < NumBanks; i++) begin
429	    assign m2s_resp.err[i]    = tmp_ersp[i][0];
430	    assign m2s_resp.exokay[i] = tmp_ersp[i][1];
431	    assign bank_ersp[i][0] = mem_err_i[i];
432	    assign bank_ersp[i][1] = mem_exokay_i[i];
433	  end
434	
435	  // Split single memory request to desired number of banks.
436	  mem_stream_to_banks_detailed #(
437	    .AddrWidth   ( AddrWidth         ),
438	    .DataWidth   ( DataWidth         ),
439	    .RUserWidth  ( 2                 ),
440	    .NumBanks    ( NumBanks          ),
441	    .HideStrb    ( HideStrb          ),
442	    .MaxTrans    ( BufDepth          ),
443	    .OutFifoDepth( OutFifoDepth      ),
444	    .WUserWidth  ( $bits(tmp_atop_t) )
445	  ) i_mem_to_banks (
446	    .clk_i,
447	    .rst_ni,
448	    .req_i         ( m2s_req_valid ),
449	    .gnt_o         ( m2s_req_ready ),
450	    .addr_i        ( m2s_req.addr  ),
451	    .wdata_i       ( m2s_req.wdata ),
452	    .strb_i        ( m2s_req.strb  ),
453	    .wuser_i       ( mem_req_atop  ),
454	    .we_i          ( m2s_req.we    ),
455	    .rvalid_o      ( m2s_resp_valid ),
456	    .rready_i      ( m2s_resp_ready ),
457	    .rdata_o       ( m2s_resp.data ),
458	    .ruser_o       ( tmp_ersp      ),
459	    .bank_req_o    ( mem_req_o     ),
460	    .bank_gnt_i    ( mem_gnt_i     ),
461	    .bank_addr_o   ( mem_addr_o    ),
462	    .bank_wdata_o  ( mem_wdata_o   ),
463	    .bank_strb_o   ( mem_strb_o    ),
464	    .bank_wuser_o  ( banked_req_atop ),
465	    .bank_we_o     ( mem_we_o      ),
466	    .bank_rvalid_i ( mem_rvalid_i  ),
467	    .bank_rdata_i  ( mem_rdata_i   ),
468	    .bank_ruser_i  ( bank_ersp     )
469	  );
470	
471	  // Join memory read data and meta data stream.
472	  logic mem_join_valid, mem_join_ready;
473	  stream_join #(
474	    .N_INP ( 32'd2 )
475	  ) i_join (
476	    .inp_valid_i  ({m2s_resp_valid, meta_buf_valid}),
477	    .inp_ready_o  ({m2s_resp_ready, meta_buf_ready}),
478	    .oup_valid_o  ( mem_join_valid                 ),
479	    .oup_ready_i  ( mem_join_ready                 )
480	  );
481	
482	  // Dynamically fork the joined stream to B and R channels.
483	  stream_fork_dynamic #(
484	    .N_OUP ( 32'd2 )
485	  ) i_fork_dynamic (
486	    .clk_i,
487	    .rst_ni,
488	    .valid_i      ( mem_join_valid                         ),
489	    .ready_o      ( mem_join_ready                         ),
490	    .sel_i        ({sel_buf_b,          sel_buf_r         }),
491	    .sel_valid_i  ( sel_buf_valid                          ),
492	    .sel_ready_o  ( sel_buf_ready                          ),
493	    .valid_o      ({axi_resp_o.b_valid, axi_resp_o.r_valid}),
494	    .ready_i      ({axi_req_i.b_ready,  axi_req_i.r_ready })
495	  );
496	
497	  localparam NumBytesPerBank = DataWidth/NumBanks/8;
498	
499	  logic [NumBanks-1:0] meta_buf_bank_strb, meta_buf_size_enable;
500	  logic resp_b_err, resp_b_exokay, resp_r_err, resp_r_exokay;
501	
502	  // Collect `err` and `exokay` from all banks
503	  // To ensure correct propagation, `err` is grouped with `OR` and `exokay` is grouped with `AND`.
504	  for (genvar i = 0; i < NumBanks; i++) begin
505	    // Set active write banks based on strobe
506	    assign meta_buf_bank_strb[i] = |meta_buf.strb[i*NumBytesPerBank +: NumBytesPerBank];
507	    // Set active read banks based on size and address offset: (bank.end > addr) && (bank.start < addr+size)
508	    assign meta_buf_size_enable[i] = ((i*NumBytesPerBank + NumBytesPerBank) > (meta_buf.addr % (DataWidth/8))) &&
509	                                     ((i*NumBytesPerBank) < ((meta_buf.addr % (DataWidth/8)) + (1<<meta_buf.size)));
510	  end
511	  assign resp_b_err    = |(m2s_resp.err    &  meta_buf_bank_strb);   // Ensure only active banks are used (strobe)
512	  assign resp_b_exokay = &(m2s_resp.exokay | ~meta_buf_bank_strb) & meta_buf.lock;   // Ensure only active banks are used (strobe)
513	  assign resp_r_err    = |(m2s_resp.err    &  meta_buf_size_enable); // Ensure only active banks are used (size & addr offset)
514	  assign resp_r_exokay = &(m2s_resp.exokay | ~meta_buf_size_enable) & meta_buf.lock; // Ensure only active banks are used (size & addr offset)
515	
516	  logic collect_b_err_d, collect_b_err_q;
517	  logic collect_b_exokay_d, collect_b_exokay_q;
518	  logic next_collect_b_err, next_collect_b_exokay;
519	
520	  // Accumulate write errors for single B response
521	  // To ensure correct propagation, `err` is grouped with `OR` and `exokay` is grouped with `AND`.
522	  assign next_collect_b_err = collect_b_err_q | resp_b_err;
523	  assign next_collect_b_exokay = collect_b_exokay_q & resp_b_exokay;
524	
525	  always_comb begin
526	    // By default we keep the previous value
527	    collect_b_err_d = collect_b_err_q;
528	    collect_b_exokay_d = collect_b_exokay_q;
529	    // If the buffer is properly popped
530	    if (sel_buf_valid && sel_buf_ready) begin
531	      if (meta_buf.write && meta_buf.last) begin
532	        collect_b_err_d = 1'b0;
533	        collect_b_exokay_d = 1'b1;
534	      end else if (meta_buf.write) begin
535	        collect_b_err_d = next_collect_b_err;
536	        collect_b_exokay_d = next_collect_b_exokay;
537	      end
538	    end
539	  end
540	
541	  // Compose B responses.
542	  assign axi_resp_o.b = '{
543	    id:   meta_buf.id,
544	    resp: next_collect_b_err ? axi_pkg::RESP_SLVERR : next_collect_b_exokay ? axi_pkg::RESP_EXOKAY : axi_pkg::RESP_OKAY,
545	    user: '0
546	  };
547	
548	  // Compose R responses.
549	  assign axi_resp_o.r = '{
550	    data: m2s_resp.data,
551	    id:   meta_buf.id,
552	    last: meta_buf.last,
553	    resp: resp_r_err ? axi_pkg::RESP_SLVERR : resp_r_exokay ? axi_pkg::RESP_EXOKAY : axi_pkg::RESP_OKAY,
554	    user: '0
555	  };
556	
557	  // Registers
558	  `FFARN(meta_sel_q, meta_sel_d, 1'b0, clk_i, rst_ni)
559	  `FFARN(sel_lock_q, sel_lock_d, 1'b0, clk_i, rst_ni)
560	  `FFARN(rd_meta_q, rd_meta_d, meta_t'{default: '0}, clk_i, rst_ni)
561	  `FFARN(wr_meta_q, wr_meta_d, meta_t'{default: '0}, clk_i, rst_ni)
562	  `FFARN(r_cnt_q, r_cnt_d, '0, clk_i, rst_ni)
563	  `FFARN(w_cnt_q, w_cnt_d, '0, clk_i, rst_ni)
564	  `FFARN(collect_b_err_q, collect_b_err_d, '0, clk_i, rst_ni)
565	  `FFARN(collect_b_exokay_q, collect_b_exokay_d, 1'b1, clk_i, rst_ni)
566	
567	  // Assertions
568	  // pragma translate_off
569	  `ifndef VERILATOR
570	  `ifndef XSIM
571	  default disable iff (!rst_ni);
572	  assume property (@(posedge clk_i)
573	      axi_req_i.ar_valid && !axi_resp_o.ar_ready |=> $stable(axi_req_i.ar))
574	    else $error("AR must remain stable until handshake has happened!");
575	  assert property (@(posedge clk_i)
576	      axi_resp_o.r_valid && !axi_req_i.r_ready |=> $stable(axi_resp_o.r))
577	    else $error("R must remain stable until handshake has happened!");
578	  assume property (@(posedge clk_i)
579	      axi_req_i.aw_valid && !axi_resp_o.aw_ready |=> $stable(axi_req_i.aw))
580	    else $error("AW must remain stable until handshake has happened!");
581	  assume property (@(posedge clk_i)
582	      axi_req_i.w_valid && !axi_resp_o.w_ready |=> $stable(axi_req_i.w))
583	    else $error("W must remain stable until handshake has happened!");
584	  assert property (@(posedge clk_i)
585	      axi_resp_o.b_valid && !axi_req_i.b_ready |=> $stable(axi_resp_o.b))
586	    else $error("B must remain stable until handshake has happened!");
587	  assert property (@(posedge clk_i) axi_req_i.ar_valid && axi_req_i.ar.len > 0 |->
588	      axi_req_i.ar.burst == axi_pkg::BURST_INCR)
589	    else $error("Non-incrementing bursts are not supported!");
590	  assert property (@(posedge clk_i) axi_req_i.aw_valid && axi_req_i.aw.len > 0 |->
591	      axi_req_i.aw.burst == axi_pkg::BURST_INCR)
592	    else $error("Non-incrementing bursts are not supported!");
593	  assert property (@(posedge clk_i) meta_valid && meta.atop != '0 |-> meta.write)
594	    else $warning("Unexpected atomic operation on read.");
595	  `endif
596	  `endif
597	  // pragma translate_on
598	endmodule
599	
600	
601	`include "axi/assign.svh"
602	`include "axi/typedef.svh"
603	/// Interface wrapper for module `axi_to_detailed_mem`.
604	module axi_to_detailed_mem_intf #(
605	  /// See `axi_to_detailed_mem`, parameter `AddrWidth`.
606	  parameter int unsigned ADDR_WIDTH     = 32'd0,
607	  /// See `axi_to_detailed_mem`, parameter `DataWidth`.
608	  parameter int unsigned DATA_WIDTH     = 32'd0,
609	  /// AXI4+ATOP ID width.
610	  parameter int unsigned ID_WIDTH       = 32'd0,
611	  /// AXI4+ATOP user width.
612	  parameter int unsigned USER_WIDTH     = 32'd0,
613	  /// See `axi_to_detailed_mem`, parameter `NumBanks`.
614	  parameter int unsigned NUM_BANKS      = 32'd0,
615	  /// See `axi_to_detailed_mem`, parameter `BufDepth`.
616	  parameter int unsigned BUF_DEPTH      = 32'd1,
617	  /// Hide write requests if the strb == '0
618	  parameter bit          HIDE_STRB      = 1'b0,
619	  /// Depth of output fifo/fall_through_register. Increase for asymmetric backpressure (contention) on banks.
620	  parameter int unsigned OUT_FIFO_DEPTH = 32'd1,
621	  /// Dependent parameter, do not override. See `axi_to_detailed_mem`, parameter `addr_t`.
622	  localparam type addr_t     = logic [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0],
623	  /// Dependent parameter, do not override. See `axi_to_detailed_mem`, parameter `mem_data_t`.
624	  localparam type mem_data_t = logic [DATA_WIDTH/NUM_BANKS-1:0],
625	  /// Dependent parameter, do not override. See `axi_to_detailed_mem`, parameter `mem_strb_t`.
626	  localparam type mem_strb_t = logic [DATA_WIDTH/NUM_BANKS/8-1:0],
627	  /// Dependent parameter, do not override. See `axi_to_detailed_mem`, parameter `mem_id_t`.
628	  localparam type mem_id_t   = logic [ID_WIDTH-1:0],
629	  /// Dependent parameter, do not override. See `axi_to_detailed_mem`, parameter `mem_user_t`.
630	  localparam type mem_user_t = logic [USER_WIDTH-1:0]
631	) (
632	  /// Clock input.
633	  input  logic                              clk_i,
634	  /// Asynchronous reset, active low.
635	  input  logic                              rst_ni,
636	  /// See `axi_to_detailed_mem`, port `busy_o`.
637	  output logic                              busy_o,
638	  /// AXI4+ATOP slave interface port.
639	  AXI_BUS.Slave                             slv,
640	  /// See `axi_to_detailed_mem`, port `mem_req_o`.
641	  output logic             [NUM_BANKS-1:0]  mem_req_o,
642	  /// See `axi_to_detailed_mem`, port `mem_gnt_i`.
643	  input  logic             [NUM_BANKS-1:0]  mem_gnt_i,
644	  /// See `axi_to_detailed_mem`, port `mem_addr_o`.
645	  output addr_t            [NUM_BANKS-1:0]  mem_addr_o,
646	  /// See `axi_to_detailed_mem`, port `mem_wdata_o`.
647	  output mem_data_t        [NUM_BANKS-1:0]  mem_wdata_o,
648	  /// See `axi_to_detailed_mem`, port `mem_strb_o`.
649	  output mem_strb_t        [NUM_BANKS-1:0]  mem_strb_o,
650	  /// See `axi_to_detailed_mem`, port `mem_atop_o`.
651	  output axi_pkg::atop_t   [NUM_BANKS-1:0]  mem_atop_o,
652	  /// See `axi_to_detailed_mem`, port `mem_lock_o`.
653	  output logic             [NUM_BANKS-1:0]  mem_lock_o,
654	  /// See `axi_to_detailed_mem`, port `mem_we_o`.
655	  output logic             [NUM_BANKS-1:0]  mem_we_o,
656	  /// See `axi_to_detailed_mem`, port `mem_id_o`.
657	  output mem_id_t          [NUM_BANKS-1:0]  mem_id_o,
658	  /// See `axi_to_detailed_mem`, port `mem_user_o`.
659	  output mem_user_t        [NUM_BANKS-1:0]  mem_user_o,
660	  /// See `axi_to_detailed_mem`, port `mem_cache_o`.
661	  output axi_pkg::cache_t  [NUM_BANKS-1:0]  mem_cache_o,
662	  /// See `axi_to_detailed_mem`, port `mem_prot_o`.
663	  output axi_pkg::prot_t   [NUM_BANKS-1:0]  mem_prot_o,
664	  /// See `axi_to_detailed_mem`, port `mem_qos_o`.
665	  output axi_pkg::qos_t    [NUM_BANKS-1:0]  mem_qos_o,
